description: Bntly — a warm, personalized intelligence layer on top of lbwalton/inbox-zero. Multi-Gmail-account support with instant onboarding scan, contact priority scoring with manual tagging, tone profile learning, silent auto-draft, Focus Mode with scheduled activation and breakthrough threshold, proactive nudge notifications (Slack, SMS, email digest, browser push), trusted sender whitelist with AI-suggested client domains, Junk label with optional auto-purge, smart label suggestions, semantic contact alias resolution, five selectable color theme palettes, and a full personalization settings hub — all under the Bntly brand.

userStories.title: Add accountLabel and isDefault fields to EmailAccount
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add user-facing fields to the existing EmailAccount model for multi-account UX.
acceptanceCriteria: Add accountLabel (String, nullable) field to EmailAccount model in prisma/schema.prisma
Add isDefault (Boolean, default false) field to EmailAccount model
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added accountLabel and isDefault fields to EmailAccount model. Prisma client generated successfully. Pre-existing test file TS errors unrelated to this change.

userStories.title: Add ContactScore table scoped to EmailAccount
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store contact priority scores per email account.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ContactScore table: id, emailAccountId, contactEmail (String), priorityScore (Float 0-100), replyRate (Float), avgReplyTimeHours (Float), manualOverride (Boolean default false), autoDraftEnabled (Boolean default true), lastUpdated (DateTime)
Add relation from EmailAccount to ContactScore
Add unique constraint on (emailAccountId, contactEmail)
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added ContactScore model with unique constraint on (emailAccountId, contactEmail). Relation added to EmailAccount.

userStories.title: Add EmailSignal table for manual tagging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store Important/Not Important manual tags per email thread.

Add relation from EmailAccount to EmailSignal
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added EmailSignal model with EmailSignalType enum (IMPORTANT/NOT_IMPORTANT).

userStories.title: Add ToneProfile table

Add relation from EmailAccount to ToneProfile
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added ToneProfile model with unique emailAccountId constraint.

userStories.title: Add NudgeLog table

acceptanceCriteria: Add NudgeLog table: id, emailAccountId, threadId (String), nudgeType (enum: OUTBOUND | INBOUND), sentAt (DateTime), status (enum: PENDING | DISMISSED | SNOOZED | ACTIONED default PENDING), snoozeUntil (DateTime nullable)
Add relation from EmailAccount to NudgeLog

userStories.notes: Added NudgeLog model with NudgeType and NudgeStatus enums.

Generate migration and apply successfully

userStories.title: Add SuggestedLabel table

userStories.title: Add FocusMode table

acceptanceCriteria: Add FocusMode table: id, userId (unique), focusedAccountId (String nullable, foreign key EmailAccount), isActive (Boolean default false), scheduleEnabled (Boolean default false), scheduleStartHour (Int nullable), scheduleEndHour (Int nullable), scheduleTimezone (String nullable), breakthroughThreshold (Int default 80), updatedAt (DateTime). Pre-existing test TS errors only.

userStories.notes: Enhanced AccountSwitcher in SideNav header with: accountLabel/isDefault display, checkmark on active account, violet dot on focused account when Focus Mode active, PUT /api/user/email-accounts/[id]/activate stub route, Add account link to /accounts. DropdownMenu from shadcn/ui provides full keyboard accessibility. Updated email-accounts API to return accountLabel, isDefault, and focusMode data.

userStories.title: Contact scoring service with EmailSignal weighting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service scoring contacts 0-100 using behavioral history and manual tags.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/contact-scoring.ts accepting emailAccountId
Base score: reply rate (35%) + inverted avg reply time normalized (25%) + thread frequency normalized (25%)
Signal: IMPORTANT tag adds +5 per tag max +15 total; NOT_IMPORTANT subtracts 10 per tag floor 0
Skip contacts where manualOverride = true
Upserts into ContactScore table
Exports: calculateContactScores(emailAccountId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created contact-scoring.ts with weighted scoring (reply rate 35%, reply time 25%, frequency 25%, signals +/-). Skips manual overrides. Upserts ContactScore.

userStories.title: Contact scoring API route and weekly cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the scoring service callable on-demand and weekly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/contact-scoring/run dispatching calculateContactScores for all EmailAccounts of authenticated user via QStash
Add weekly cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/contact-scoring/run", "schedule": "0 2 * * 0" }
Returns { queued: true } immediately
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Manual email tagging API route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a route that saves a signal tag and re-triggers scoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/emails/signal accepting { threadId, senderEmail, signal: 'IMPORTANT' | 'NOT_IMPORTANT', emailAccountId }
Upserts EmailSignal record (replaces existing signal for same threadId if present)
Dispatches calculateContactScores for that emailAccountId via QStash
Returns { saved: true }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created POST /api/emails/signal with upsert and QStash scoring dispatch.

userStories.title: Tone profile scanner service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that extracts a writing tone fingerprint from 90 days of sent mail.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/tone-scanner.ts accepting emailAccountId
Reads last 90 days of sent mail via Gmail API using that account's credentials
Uses configured LLM (existing abstraction) to extract: avgSentenceLength, top 5 openers, top 5 sign-offs, formalityScore 1-5, top 10 common phrases
Upserts into ToneProfile table
Exports: scanToneProfile(emailAccountId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created tone-scanner.ts using Gmail API + LLM abstraction. Extracts openers, signoffs, formality, phrases. Upserts ToneProfile.

userStories.title: Tone profile API routes and monthly cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need routes to get, update, and trigger tone scans.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tone-profile returns ToneProfile for active emailAccountId
PUT /api/tone-profile accepts partial updates for active account
POST /api/tone-profile/scan dispatches scanToneProfile for all email accounts via QStash
Add monthly cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/tone-profile/scan", "schedule": "0 3 1 * *" }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-draft generation service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that silently creates Gmail draft replies for high-priority inbound emails.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/auto-draft.ts accepting emailAccountId, threadId, inboundEmailBody
Returns early if ContactScore for sender is below autoDraftThreshold or autoDraftEnabled = false for that contact
Fetches ToneProfile for emailAccountId to use as LLM system context
Calls LLM via existing abstraction with full thread history as user message
Creates draft via gmail.drafts.create using account credentials — never sends
Exports: generateAutoDraft(emailAccountId: string, threadId: string, emailBody: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Hook auto-draft and inbox filter into PubSub webhook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the PubSub webhook to identify the correct EmailAccount and dispatch per-email jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: In existing /api/google/webhook, identify EmailAccount by matching Gmail address from PubSub message
Dispatch generateAutoDraft via QStash if user autoDraftEnabled = true
Dispatch filterInboundEmail via QStash for every inbound email
Both dispatches are non-blocking — webhook returns 200 immediately
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Outbound and inbound nudge detection services
userStories.description: As a developer, I need two services detecting stale outbound emails and unreplied inbound emails.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/outbound-nudge.ts: scans sent mail for trigger phrases ('let me know', 'please confirm', 'can you', 'do you have', 'following up', 'waiting to hear', 'please respond', 'your thoughts'), checks for replies, creates NudgeLog type OUTBOUND if no reply after outboundNudgeDays and no NudgeLog for threadId within 24hrs. Exports: detectOutboundNudges(emailAccountId: string).

Create /apps/web/utils/inbound-nudge.ts: scans inbox for unreplied emails older than inboundNudgeDays, uses LLM to classify 'Does this email expect a reply? yes or no', creates NudgeLog type INBOUND if yes and no duplicate within 24hrs. Exports: detectInboundNudges(emailAccountId: string).
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Nudge detection daily cron route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need both nudge detection services to run daily across all email accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/nudge/detect dispatching detectOutboundNudges and detectInboundNudges for every EmailAccount of all users via QStash
Add daily cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/nudge/detect", "schedule": "0 12 * * *" }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Focus Mode enforcement logic in nudge dispatcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the nudge dispatcher to suppress cross-account notifications below the breakthrough threshold when Focus Mode is active.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/focus-mode-check.ts that accepts userId and nudge emailAccountId and returns: shouldSuppress (boolean)
Logic: if FocusMode.isActive = false, shouldSuppress = false always
If FocusMode.scheduleEnabled = true, compute whether current time in scheduleTimezone is within scheduleStartHour to scheduleEndHour; if outside window, shouldSuppress = false
If active and nudge emailAccountId != focusedAccountId, fetch ContactScore for nudge sender; if priorityScore < breakthroughThreshold, shouldSuppress = true
If active and nudge emailAccountId == focusedAccountId, shouldSuppress = false
Exports: shouldSuppressNudge(userId: string, nudgeEmailAccountId: string, senderEmail: string): Promise<boolean>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack notification utility

acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/notify-slack.ts: POST to SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with account label, nudge type, sender, subject, date, link. Only if slackEnabled = true.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SMS notification utility

userStories.title: Email digest API route and daily cron

userStories.title: Nudge dispatcher orchestrator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single orchestrator that reads pending NudgeLogs, applies Focus Mode suppression, and dispatches to all enabled channels.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/nudge-dispatcher.ts
Reads all PENDING NudgeLog records for user across all accounts
For each nudge, calls shouldSuppressNudge — if true, skip dispatch for this nudge
If not suppressed, dispatches to Slack/SMS/push based on user settings
Updates each NudgeLog status to ACTIONED after dispatch
Create POST /api/nudge/dispatch that triggers the dispatcher via QStash

userStories.title: Trusted sender inbox filter service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need logic that archives unknown-sender emails and routes spam to Junk, incorporating EmailSignal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/inbox-filter.ts accepting emailAccountId and sender email
Trusted if: in TrustedSender (case-insensitive contact or domain match, addedManually = true or false) OR in ContactScore OR EmailSignal has IMPORTANT for sender
NOT_IMPORTANT signal overrides trust — treated as unknown regardless
Trusted: leave in inbox; Unknown: apply label 'Review', remove INBOX label; Spam/cold (reuse base app logic): apply label 'Junk'
Create 'Review' and 'Junk' Gmail labels if they don't exist (check first, create if missing)
Exports: filterInboundEmail(emailAccountId: string, senderEmail: string, threadId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Junk auto-purge service, API route, and weekly cron

Create POST /api/junk/purge route dispatching junk-purge for all EmailAccounts via QStash
Add weekly cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/junk/purge", "schedule": "0 4 * * 1" }

userStories.title: Smart label suggestion service and monthly cron

userStories.description: As LB, I want to view and override contact scores and per-contact auto-draft settings.

Fetches top 50 ContactScore records for active account sorted by priorityScore desc
Table columns: Email, Score badge (gray 0-39 / amber 40-79 / green 80-100), Reply Rate (%), Avg Reply Time, Auto-Draft toggle, Manual Override lock icon

userStories.title: Enable pgvector and add ContactEmbedding and ContactAlias tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need vector storage and alias storage in Supabase to power semantic contact resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable pgvector extension in Supabase via raw SQL migration: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

Add ContactEmbedding table via Prisma using Unsupported type for the vector column: id, emailAccountId, contactEmail (String), contactName (String nullable), embedding Unsupported('vector(1536)'), updatedAt (DateTime). Unique constraint on (emailAccountId, contactEmail).
Add ContactAlias table: id, userId, phrase (String), resolvedEmail (String), resolvedName (String nullable), emailAccountId (String), confirmedAt (DateTime). Unique constraint on (userId, phrase).
Add relations: EmailAccount to ContactEmbedding (one-to-many), User to ContactAlias (one-to-many)
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Contact embedding generation service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that generates vector embeddings for all contacts in an email account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/contact-embedder.ts accepting emailAccountId
Fetches all contacts from Gmail Contacts API (name + email) for the account
Also fetches top 100 ContactScore records for that account to include senders not in Contacts
For each contact, constructs embedding input: '[name] [email] [any labels from TrustedSender or ContactScore]'
Calls OpenAI Embeddings API using text-embedding-3-small model (1536 dimensions) or configured LLM provider's embedding endpoint
Upserts each embedding into ContactEmbedding table
Dispatched via QStash on new account connect alongside calculateContactScores and scanToneProfile
Exports: generateContactEmbeddings(emailAccountId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Semantic alias resolution service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that takes a natural language phrase and returns the best-matching contact using pgvector similarity search.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/alias-resolver.ts
First checks ContactAlias table for exact phrase match (case-insensitive) — if found, return immediately with confirmed: true, skip vector search
If no confirmed alias: embed the input phrase using the same embedding model as US-056
Run pgvector cosine similarity search via raw Prisma query against ContactEmbedding for all email accounts of the user: ORDER BY embedding <=> $queryVector LIMIT 3
Return top 3 matches as array of { contactEmail, contactName, similarityScore, emailAccountId }
Exports: resolveAlias(userId: string, phrase: string): Promise<AliasMatch[]>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Alias confirmation API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API routes to resolve, confirm, list, update, and delete contact aliases.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/alias/resolve accepting { phrase }: calls resolveAlias and returns top 3 matches. If exact confirmed alias exists, returns it with confirmed: true.
POST /api/alias/confirm accepting { phrase, resolvedEmail, resolvedName, emailAccountId }: upserts ContactAlias record. Returns { saved: true }.
GET /api/alias: returns all ContactAlias records for authenticated user
DELETE /api/alias/:id: deletes a stored alias
PATCH /api/alias/:id accepting { resolvedEmail, resolvedName }: updates a stored alias to a different contact
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Alias resolution inline UI in email compose To: field
userStories.description: As LB, I want the compose To: field to suggest a real contact when I type a natural language phrase, and remember my choice going forward.
acceptanceCriteria: In the compose/reply To: field, after user pauses typing (300ms debounce), call POST /api/alias/resolve if the input does not look like an email address (no @ symbol)
If a confirmed alias is returned: silently replace the typed phrase with the resolved contact chip (name + email) — no confirmation prompt
If unconfirmed matches returned: render an inline confirmation chip below the To: field showing contact avatar initial, resolved name, email, 'Use this' button, and 'Not right' link
'Use this': calls POST /api/alias/confirm then replaces typed phrase with contact chip
'Not right': dismisses chip and shows next best match if available
Confirmation chip animates in: opacity 0 to 1 + translateY 4px to 0, spring mass 0.4 / stiffness 450 / damping 28
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
